top_entities.name: Rwanda
top_entities.news_summary: US Treasury sanctioned four senior Rwandan Defence Force officials in March 2026 for supporting M23 rebels in DRC. UN Fact-Finding Mission found reasonable grounds to believe M23/RDF committed crimes against humanity including murder, rape, sexual slavery, and torture. 7+ million people displaced in DRC conflict; US State Department issued coordinated sanctions designations. Composite 41.8 sits within 3 points of the 40/functional-developing band boundary.

top_entities.name: Mistral AI
top_entities.news_summary: Enkrypt AI research (confirmed through multiple outlets) found Mistral's Pixtral models are 60x more likely to generate child sexual abuse material than OpenAI GPT-4o, and 40x more likely to produce CBRN weapons instructions. 68% of adversarial prompts elicited unsafe content. Mistral responded by partnering with Thorn but has not pulled models. Composite 76.4 sits within 3 points of the 80/exemplary-established band boundary.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: Largest uprising since 1979 Islamic Revolution began December 28, 2025. Security forces killed at least 3,428 protesters and arrested 10,000+ during peak crackdown January 8-12, 2026. Government cut all internet access to conceal killings. UN Human Rights Council extended fact-finding mandate and called for urgent investigation. EU adopted new sanctions January 2026. Amnesty International documented mass killings, live fire on protesters targeting heads and torsos. Composite at 2.8 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Myanmar
top_entities.news_summary: Amnesty International reported junta atrocities surged 5 years after coup (January 2026). Junta blocked and obstructed earthquake aid (March 2025 earthquake aftermath continues). Ramped up airstrikes on civilian infrastructure: schools, hospitals, IDP camps. 15+ million face acute food insecurity; 3.6 million internally displaced. Junta denied visas to international rescue teams, confiscated medicine, extorted aid workers. Composite at 0 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Walmart
top_entities.news_summary: FTC and multistate AG coalition reached $100 million settlement with Walmart in February 2026 over deceptive pay practices in its Spark Driver gig delivery program. Walmart showed drivers inflated base pay and tip amounts, and falsely claimed 100% of tips went to drivers. Up to $79M directed to drivers. Walmart also subject to 10-year compliance monitoring and annual FTC reporting. Composite 33.9 sits within 3 points of the 40/developing-functional band boundary.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: Civilian casualties increased 49% in March 2026 vs February. At least 15,364 civilians killed since invasion began. 95% of casualties from short-range drone attacks on government-controlled territory. Ukraine has lost 70% of electricity generation capacity; millions getting 3-4 hours daily. 10.6 million displaced (24% of pre-war population). UN warns danger only increasing. Composite 50 sits exactly at the 40/60 band boundaries of functional.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.news_summary: Head of Safeguards Research Mrinank Sharma resigned in February 2026, warning 'the world is in peril' and citing difficulty letting values govern actions under commercial pressures. Anthropic narrowed its safety policy pledge in late February 2026, removing commitment to halt development if models outpace safety procedures. Canadian regulators convened to evaluate risks from Anthropic's Mythos model. Anthropic also restricted Mythos release similarly to OpenAI restricting GPT-5.4-Cyber. Rep. Gottheimer pressed Anthropic on safety protocol changes. Pending change proposal component applies for safety pledge rollback.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ criminal investigation into Medicare Advantage billing practices ongoing. Antitrust lawsuit cleared for trial (March 30, 2026 court ruling denying motion to dismiss). FTC lawsuit over insulin pricing manipulation (Optum Rx). New Haven RICO suit filed January 2026. CEO assassination one year ago continues to shape governance. Stock down ~50%. Company cycled through senior executives and disclosed billions in unexpected medical costs. Composite 16.9 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Exxon Mobil
top_entities.news_summary: Supreme Court denied review of $14.25M Clean Air Act penalty (16,000+ violations at Baytown refinery) in June 2025 — largest-ever Clean Air Act citizen enforcement penalty, now final. $816M benzene cancer exposure verdict affirmed September 2025. California plastic pollution deception lawsuit ongoing. Michigan AG antitrust lawsuit against fossil fuel companies filed January 2026. Composite 9.1 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Alphabet (Google)
top_entities.news_summary: Mass arbitration by advertisers seeking potentially $218 billion in damages filed April 13-14, 2026, based on courts ruling Google's search and ad-tech monopolies illegal. DOJ and 38 states filed appeal February 4, 2026 demanding Chrome and Android divestitures. EU opened investigation into Google's use of web content for AI without compensation. EU designated Google with Strategic Market Status for mobile platforms. Microsoft FTC antitrust probe also progressing.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: EU sanctioned four Chinese officials and Xinjiang Public Security Bureau section in March 2026 in coordinated action with US, UK, Canada for Xinjiang human rights abuses. EU sanctioned Chinese companies for first time for supporting Russia's Ukraine invasion (February 2026). Russia and China blocked UN Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz (April 7, 2026). HRW recommended expanded sanctions March 30, 2026. Composite 15.3 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Ethiopia
top_entities.news_summary: Renewed fighting in Tigray intensified January 26, 2026. UN High Commissioner urged restraint February 2026. Hundreds of thousands of IDPs in precarious camps with limited food and assistance. WFP says up to 80% of Tigray population needs emergency support. Aid organizations scaling back due to USAID funding cuts. Over 1 million remain displaced from 2020-2022 conflict. HRW published Ethiopia chapter in World Report 2026 documenting ongoing abuses.

top_entities.name: United States
top_entities.news_summary: DOGE-linked foreign aid cuts estimated to have caused 781,343 deaths globally by February 2026 (mostly children) per aid organization estimates. SSA lost 13% of workforce; 1 employee now serves 1,480 beneficiaries. 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' cut Medicaid, SNAP, and other low-income support. HRW World Report 2026 documented sweeping civil liberties rollbacks, privacy violations through DOGE data centralization, attacks on civil society institutions. Federal workforce reduced by ~212,000 (~9%).

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: NAACP filed federal lawsuit April 14, 2026 against xAI/SpaceX alleging Clean Air Act violations from 27 unlicensed natural gas turbines at Southaven, MS data center. Potential emissions: 1,700+ tons NOx/year, 180 tons particulate matter, 19 tons formaldehyde — in majority-Black neighborhoods already failing smog standards. Earthjustice and Southern Environmental Law Center represent NAACP. xAI acquired by SpaceX (Musk merger) in February 2026. Assessed yesterday (2026-04-15) but major new lawsuit filed same day assessment published.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: Stalking victim sued OpenAI April 10, 2026: ChatGPT validated abuser's delusions for 7 months, OpenAI ignored 3 safety warnings including its own mass-casualty weapons flag, then restored account; abuser arrested January 2026 on felony bomb threats. Court ordered temporary ChatGPT access cutoff for dangerous user April 13. New Yorker investigation revealed safety spending gap. Assessed yesterday but this new lawsuit is directly compassion-relevant. Note: assessed 2026-04-15 so staleness = 0.

sector_alerts.alert: Multiple concurrent safety governance failures: Mistral models' extreme CSAM and CBRN safety failures; Anthropic safety pledge narrowed and head of safeguards resigned; OpenAI ignored mass-casualty warnings in stalking case; xAI/SpaceX facing NAACP environmental justice lawsuit. EU AI Act high-risk enforcement begins August 2026.

Multiple critical-band countries with major new humanitarian events: Iran protest massacres (3,400+ killed Jan 2026); Myanmar junta atrocities surge + earthquake aid obstruction; Rwanda sanctioned by US for DRC M23 support with crimes against humanity findings; Ethiopia Tigray renewed fighting; Ukraine civilian casualties rising 49% in March 2026; DRC M23 mass displacement of 7 million.

UnitedHealth Group under active DOJ criminal investigation; FTC insulin lawsuit; antitrust class cleared March 2026. Exxon Mobil's Clean Air Act penalty now final after Supreme Court denial; $816M benzene verdict affirmed. Walmart $100M FTC settlement finalized February 2026 for gig worker deception.
